input.headline: No place for Mostert distraction in South Africa team

raw.headline: No place for Mostert distraction in South Africa team

winner: Mostert tried to join the South Africa team during a training session, but the coach said, 'No place for you here!' The poor guy was so confused—he thought he was supposed to bring a chair.

candidates.text: Why did the South African player bring a ladder to the match? Because he wanted to make sure there was no *place* for distractions like Mostert!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'place' and 'ladder' are clever and specific, ties well to the headline, and is concise with a surprising twist."}

candidates.text: Why did the rugby team in South Africa refuse to play cards? Because they didn't want any "Mostert" distractions.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and ties to the headline, but 'Mostert' as a distraction in cards is not clearly explained, reducing surprise and clarity."}

candidates.text: Mostert was so distracted in training that he forgot his own name—but when he finally remembered, he scored the winning try and said, 'That’s my middle name.' The coaches were like, 'No place for *that* distraction in our team.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific sports reference, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: The South African team told Mostert there was no room for distractions in the lineup, which was a shame because he was planning on being the main course.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'main course' add a surprising and specific twist, aligning well with the headline's context."}

candidates.text: I told my dog he was the most important thing in my life — now he's sitting on the couch and won't move.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relatable and has a mild twist, but it doesn't directly connect to the headline about Mostert or South Africa team, reducing its relevance and surprise factor."}

candidates.text: Mostert tried to join the South Africa team during a training session, but the coach said, 'No place for you here!' The poor guy was so confused—he thought he was supposed to bring a chair.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the headline, but the humor is weak and relies on a forced misunderstanding. The 'chair' punchline feels generic and doesn't strongly tie back to the context of Mostert being a distraction."}

candidates.text: The South African team tried to bench Mostert, but they couldn't find any room because he was already occupying the entire locker room as a distraction.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a clever play on 'distraction' in a football context."}

candidates.text: The South African team decided to remove all mirrors from their vehicles so that Mostert wouldn't see his own face and get distracted.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and uses a clever twist on the idea of distraction, but it feels slightly forced and lacks natural flow. The reference to 'Mostert' may not be widely understood without context."}

candidates.text: The coach told Mostert that there was no room for distractions, which is a shame because he was really hoping for some "most-ert" important attention.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses a pun on 'Mostert' and 'most-ert,' which is clever, but the setup could be tighter for maximum impact. It's somewhat generic in its approach and lacks deeper specificity to the context of South Africa's team or Mostert's role."}
